result.summary: identitytoolkit.googleapis.com exposes two REST API surfaces: Identity Toolkit v1 and v2 over HTTPS; both require authentication, with v1 supporting API key or Google OAuth 2.0 access token on some methods and v2 using Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
result.description: Google Identity Platform's Identity Toolkit service provides authentication and user-management APIs for applications, including sign-in flows, account operations, tenant management, and project configuration. The service is hosted at identitytoolkit.googleapis.com as versioned REST endpoints.

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key for Identity Platform
gcp_api_key.setup: In [Google Cloud Console Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials), create or select an API key for the project that has Identity Platform enabled. The Identity Platform guide says: "To use the REST API, you'll need an Identity Platform API key." You pass it as the `key` query parameter to client-facing Identity Toolkit `v1` endpoints.

google_oauth_token.label: Google OAuth 2.0 access token
google_oauth_token.setup: Use a Google Cloud credential that can mint OAuth 2.0 access tokens, such as a service account or OAuth client in [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials). Then obtain an access token with scopes required by the method, commonly `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` and sometimes `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/firebase`, as listed in the Identity Toolkit discovery document and method reference pages. For server-to-server use, the easiest path is usually a service account from [IAM & Admin service accounts](https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/serviceaccounts).
